{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/39699","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/39699/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/39699/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/39699/events","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/39699","id":809912985,"node_id":"MDU6SXNzdWU4MDk5MTI5ODU=","number":39699,"title":"EXCEPTION_ACCESS_VIOLATION when interrupting a non-interactive readline","user":{"login":"Maurdekye","id":2313721,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIzMTM3MjE=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/2313721?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/Maurdekye","html_url":"https://github.com/Maurdekye","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Maurdekye/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Maurdekye/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Maurdekye/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Maurdekye/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Maurdekye/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Maurdekye/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Maurdekye/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Maurdekye/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Maurdekye/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":2,"created_at":"2021-02-17T06:53:34Z","updated_at":"2024-09-23T15:31:44Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"Minimum example require to recreate:
Create a Julia file with the line `readline()`, run it directly from the command line with `julia read_bug.jl`, and attempt to interrupt it with Ctrl+C. Intead of throwing an `InterruptException`, as it does in an interactive session, it produces this memory access violation:

```
Please submit a bug report with steps to reproduce this fault, and any error messages that follow (in their entirety). Thanks.
Exception: EXCEPTION_ACCESS_VIOLATION at 0x668c70ea -- jl_mutex_unlock at /cygdrive/d/buildbot/worker/package_win64/build/src\locks.h:143 [inlined]
jl_get_binding_ at /cygdrive/d/buildbot/worker/package_win64/build/src\module.c:294
in expression starting at C:\Users\<me>\Desktop\read_bug.jl:1
```

Tested with Julia 1.5.1 on Windows 10 64-bit. I though `julia -i` was only for REPL interaction.

Seeing now that if I am planning on blocking with user input on `readline()` and supporting user input like `Ctrl-C` to cancel out in my script, then I need to enable the interactive mode, even though I don't need the REPL.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2368653754/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/45170","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/45170/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/45170/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/45170/events","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/45170","id":1224212929,"node_id":"I_kwDOABkWpM5I-AHB","number":45170,"title":"A precompiled closed channel isopen.","user":{"login":"peteristhegreat","id":2342284,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIzNDIyODQ=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/2342284?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/peteristhegreat","html_url":"https://github.com/peteristhegreat","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/peteristhegreat/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/peteristhegreat/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/peteristhegreat/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/peteristhegreat/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/peteristhegreat/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/peteristhegreat/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/peteristhegreat/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/peteristhegreat/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/peteristhegreat/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2022-05-03T14:54:54Z","updated_at":"2022-05-03T14:54:54Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"A channel initialized as `closed` misbehaves if initialized in open code.

Create a quick environment for seeing the issue:
```
]generate ChannelTest
open("ChannelTest/src/ChannelTest.jl", "w") do f
       write(f, """
           module ChannelTest
    
           global ch1 = Channel(x->nothing)
    
           function __init__()
               global ch2 = Channel(x->nothing)
           end

           end # module
       """)
end
]activate ChannelTest
```

Here is the demonstration of the behavior
```
julia> using ChannelTest
[ Info: Precompiling ChannelTest [faef8667-0211-41c9-aee8-e4c24749ae0b]

julia> isopen(ChannelTest.ch1)
true                                              # This is the one initialized in open code in the module

julia> isopen(ChannelTest.ch2)
false                                             # This is the one initialized in the __init__ function
```

(We have be moving away from creating anything besides functions and references in open code.)

Can you please:
 - Either fix the behavior for this specific kind of use of Channels, or 
 - Add big warning to the user about how it won't behave the way they expect.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/45170/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/45170/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null}}

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/45170","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/45170/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/45170/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/45170/events","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/45170","id":1224212929,"node_id":"I_kwDOABkWpM5I-AHB","number":45170,"title":"A precompiled closed channel isopen.","user":{"login":"peteristhegreat","id":2342284,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIzNDIyODQ=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/2342284?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/peteristhegreat","html_url":"https://github.com/peteristhegreat","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/peteristhegreat/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/peteristhegreat/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/peteristhegreat/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/peteristhegreat/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/peteristhegreat/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/peteristhegreat/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/peteristhegreat/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/peteristhegreat/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/peteristhegreat/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":3,"created_at":"2022-05-03T14:54:54Z","updated_at":"2022-05-25T15:03:54Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"A channel initialized as `closed` misbehaves if initialized in open code.

Create a quick environment for seeing the issue:
```
]generate ChannelTest
open("ChannelTest/src/ChannelTest.jl", "w") do f
       write(f, """
           module ChannelTest
    
           global ch1 = Channel(x->nothing)
    
           function __init__()
               global ch2 = Channel(x->nothing)
           end

           end # module
       """)
end
]activate ChannelTest
```

Here is the demonstration of the behavior
```
julia> using ChannelTest
[ Info: Precompiling ChannelTest [faef8667-0211-41c9-aee8-e4c24749ae0b]

julia> isopen(ChannelTest.ch1)
true                                              # This is the one initialized in open code in the module

julia> isopen(ChannelTest.ch2)
false                                             # This is the one initialized in the __init__ function
```

(We have been moving away from creating anything besides functions and references in open code.)

Can you please:
 - Either fix the behavior for this specific kind of use of Channels, or 
 - Add big warning to the user about how it won't behave the way they expect.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/45170/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/45170/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1137399046","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/45170#issuecomment-1137399046","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/45170","id":1137399046,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM5Dy1UG","user":{"login":"peteristhegreat","id":2342284,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIzNDIyODQ=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/2342284?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/peteristhegreat","html_url":"https://github.com/peteristhegreat","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/peteristhegreat/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/peteristhegreat/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/peteristhegreat/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/peteristhegreat/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/peteristhegreat/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/peteristhegreat/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/peteristhegreat/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/peteristhegreat/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/peteristhegreat/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2022-05-25T15:03:53Z","updated_at":"2022-05-25T15:03:53Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"Thanks @JeffBezanson for the replies.

For now we just initialize the channel at runtime.  But there ought to be some kind of warning for delayed channel close in open code.

I don't know the answer or what would be best for our use case, since we have opted to avoid the situation if possible.

Some other possible ideas from what you described...

a default exit task handler (during the precompiling process)
 - prints a warning and line of code that caused it, and continues with the drop of the task
 
an alternative exit task handler (during the precompiling process)
 - waits until a timeout or all tasks have exited, if a timeout exit occurs, print the warning

Or maybe just tuning a timeout value may be enough? `--precompile-task-exit-timeout` 
 - `-1` never timeout, let the tasks complete
 - `0` exit tasks immediately
 - `XX` number of seconds to wait before exiting still open tasks
 
If a warning about an exited task ought to have a silent mode, then that could be another argument.
 - `--precompile-task-exit-warning=true`
 - `--precompile-task-exit-warning=false`","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1137399046/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}
